688	    M \otimes_X (N \otimes_X P)
689	  \]
690	  in \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\). This is an \emph{objectwise}
691	  existence-of-isomorphism statement for the three fixed locally trivial objects
692	  \(M, N, P\) --- not a natural associator on a category --- and that is
693	  exactly the datum consumed by the group law on \(\otimes\)-iso-classes
694	  (\cref{lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup}), where associativity is the
695	  proposition \(\mathrm{Nonempty}\bigl((M \otimes_X N) \otimes_X P \cong
696	  M \otimes_X (N \otimes_X P)\bigr)\). No pentagon identity is asserted.
697	\end{lemma}
698	
699	\begin{proof}
700	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj, def:IsLocallyTrivial,
701	        lem:isiso_sheafification_map_of_W}
702	  The associator is built directly from the presheaf-of-modules monoidal data by
703	  absorbing the two nested sheafifications. The only non-formal input is that the
704	  whiskered sheafification unit lies in the sheafification localizer class
705	  \(J.W\); this is proved at the presheaf/section level on a trivialising cover,
706	  using local triviality of the whiskering object and no flatness hypothesis.
707	
708	  \emph{Notation.} Write \(a = \mathtt{PresheafOfModules.sheafification}\) for the
709	  sheafification functor and \(M^{\flat}\) for the underlying presheaf of an
710	  object \(M\), so that the substrate of \cref{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj} is
711	  \(M \otimes_X N = a\bigl(M^{\flat} \otimes^{p} N^{\flat}\bigr)\), with
712	  \(\otimes^{p}\) the presheaf-of-modules tensor product
713	  (\texttt{Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Monoidal.PresheafOfModules}). Let
714	  \(\eta = \mathtt{toSheafify}\) be the sheafification unit, the canonical
715	  morphism \(A \to a(A)^{\flat}\); it lies in the sheafification localizer class
716	  \(J.W\), being locally injective (\(\mathtt{isLocallyInjective\_toSheafify}\))
717	  and locally surjective. Let \(\alpha\) be the associator of the
718	  presheaf-of-modules monoidal category,
719	  \[
720	    \alpha \;\colon\;
721	      (M^{\flat} \otimes^{p} N^{\flat}) \otimes^{p} P^{\flat}
722	        \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;
723	      M^{\flat} \otimes^{p} (N^{\flat} \otimes^{p} P^{\flat}).
724	  \]
725	
726	  \emph{The key claim: a whiskered unit stays in \(J.W\).} We show that for a
727	  \emph{locally trivial} object \(F\) and any presheaf \(A\) the right-whiskered
728	  unit \(\eta_A \triangleright F^{\flat}\) lies in \(J.W\) (and symmetrically the
729	  left-whiskered unit \(F^{\flat} \triangleleft \eta_A\)). By the locally-bijective
730	  characterisation of \(J.W\) it suffices to check local surjectivity and local
731	  injectivity.
732	
733	  \emph{Local surjectivity is free.} The presheaf tensor is right exact, so
734	  whiskering carries the (locally zero) cokernel of \(\eta_A\) to the cokernel of
735	  \(\eta_A \triangleright F^{\flat}\); hence the whiskered map is locally
736	  surjective (\(\mathtt{isLocallySurjective\_whiskerLeft}\)), with no hypothesis on
737	  \(F\).
738	
739	  \emph{Local injectivity is local on the target, and is supplied by the
740	  trivialising cover.} Local injectivity asks that the equalizer sieve of the
741	  morphism be a covering sieve; this is a \emph{local-on-target} condition, so it
742	  may be checked after passing to a cover. Since \(F\) is locally trivial
743	  (\cref{def:IsLocallyTrivial}), there is an open cover \(\{V \hookrightarrow U\}\)
744	  of each open \(U\) on which \(F\) restricts to the structure sheaf,
745	  \(F^{\flat}|_V \cong \mathcal{O}_X|_V\) --- a sectionwise consequence of the
746	  trivialisation \(F|_V \cong \mathcal{O}_X|_V\) furnished by
747	  \(\mathtt{restrictIsoUnitOfLE}\). The presheaf tensor product is
748	  sectionwise/definitional,
749	  \[
750	    (A \otimes^{p} F^{\flat}).\mathrm{obj}\,V
751	      \;=\; A.\mathrm{obj}\,V \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X(V)} F^{\flat}(V),
752	  \]
753	  so over \(V\) the trivialisation \(F^{\flat}(V) \cong \mathcal{O}_X(V)\) and the
754	  right unitor \(\rho\) of the presheaf monoidal structure carry
755	  \((\eta_A \triangleright F^{\flat})|_V\) onto \(\eta_A|_V\) itself. Now
756	  \(\eta_A = \mathtt{toSheafify}\) is locally injective
757	  (\(\mathtt{isLocallyInjective\_toSheafify}\)), hence so is its restriction to the
758	  cover; gluing the per-\(V\) injectivity along the cover, \(\eta_A \triangleright
759	  F^{\flat}\) is locally injective. (The sieve bookkeeping is exactly that of the
760	  flat-whiskering injectivity argument
761	  \(\mathtt{isLocallyInjective\_whiskerLeft\_of\_flat}\); its
762	  \(\mathtt{Module.Flat.lTensor\_exact}\) injectivity step is the only thing
763	  swapped --- replaced here by the trivialisation
764	  \(F^{\flat}(V) \cong \mathcal{O}_X(V)\) on the cover and the right unitor.) The
765	  left-whiskered statement \(F^{\flat} \triangleleft \eta_A\) is identical with the
766	  left unitor \(\lambda\) in place of \(\rho\).
767	
768	  Local injectivity together with local surjectivity gives
769	  \(\eta_A \triangleright F^{\flat} \in J.W\) (resp.\ \(F^{\flat} \triangleleft
770	  \eta_A \in J.W\)). Applying the closed bridge
771	  \cref{lem:isiso_sheafification_map_of_W}, the sheafification \(a\) sends each such
772	  whiskered unit to an isomorphism.
773	
774	  \emph{The nested form.} Unfolding the substrate on both sides exposes an inner
775	  sheafification:
776	  \[
777	    (M \otimes_X N) \otimes_X P
778	      \;=\; a\Bigl(\, a\bigl(M^{\flat} \otimes^{p} N^{\flat}\bigr)^{\flat}
779	              \otimes^{p} P^{\flat} \,\Bigr),
780	    \qquad
781	    M \otimes_X (N \otimes_X P)
782	      \;=\; a\Bigl(\, M^{\flat} \otimes^{p}
783	              a\bigl(N^{\flat} \otimes^{p} P^{\flat}\bigr)^{\flat} \,\Bigr).
784	  \]
785	  The required isomorphism is the three-step composite:
786	  \begin{enumerate}
787	    \item \emph{Absorb the inner sheafification on the left.} The unit
788	      \(\eta_{M^{\flat} \otimes^{p} N^{\flat}}\) lies in \(J.W\); its
789	      right-whiskering by the locally trivial object \(P\),
790	      \(\eta_{M^{\flat} \otimes^{p} N^{\flat}} \triangleright P^{\flat}\), again
791	      lies in \(J.W\) by the key claim above (\(P\) locally trivial). By
792	      \cref{lem:isiso_sheafification_map_of_W} the sheafification functor \(a\)
793	      inverts it:
794	      \[
795	        a\Bigl(\, a\bigl(M^{\flat} \otimes^{p} N^{\flat}\bigr)^{\flat}
796	              \otimes^{p} P^{\flat} \,\Bigr)
797	          \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;
798	        a\bigl(\, (M^{\flat} \otimes^{p} N^{\flat}) \otimes^{p} P^{\flat} \,\bigr).
799	      \]
800	    \item \emph{Transport the presheaf associator.} Apply \(a\) to \(\alpha\):
801	      \[
802	        a\bigl(\, (M^{\flat} \otimes^{p} N^{\flat}) \otimes^{p} P^{\flat} \,\bigr)
803	          \;\xrightarrow[\ \sim\ ]{\;a.\mathtt{mapIso}\,\alpha\;}\;
804	        a\bigl(\, M^{\flat} \otimes^{p} (N^{\flat} \otimes^{p} P^{\flat}) \,\bigr).
805	      \]
806	    \item \emph{Restore the inner sheafification on the right.} The unit
807	      \(\eta_{N^{\flat} \otimes^{p} P^{\flat}}\) lies in \(J.W\); its
808	      left-whiskering by the locally trivial object \(M\),
809	      \(M^{\flat} \triangleleft \eta_{N^{\flat} \otimes^{p} P^{\flat}}\), again
810	      lies in \(J.W\) by the key claim above (\(M\) locally trivial). By
811	      \cref{lem:isiso_sheafification_map_of_W} \(a\) inverts it:
812	      \[
813	        a\bigl(\, M^{\flat} \otimes^{p} (N^{\flat} \otimes^{p} P^{\flat}) \,\bigr)
814	          \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;
815	        a\Bigl(\, M^{\flat} \otimes^{p}
816	              a\bigl(N^{\flat} \otimes^{p} P^{\flat}\bigr)^{\flat} \,\Bigr)
817	          \;=\; M \otimes_X (N \otimes_X P).
818	      \]
819	  \end{enumerate}
820	  Composing the three isomorphisms yields
821	  \((M \otimes_X N) \otimes_X P \xrightarrow{\sim} M \otimes_X (N \otimes_X P)\).
822	  Only steps 1 and 3 carry non-formal content, namely the key claim that the
823	  whiskered unit lies in \(J.W\); step 2 is the image under \(a\) of the presheaf
824	  monoidal associator already supplied by Mathlib.

(iter-212 finding). The associator only ever whiskers the sheafification UNIT

local bijectivity with NO flatness hypothesis: stalkwise `(F ◁ g)_x = id_{F_x}
⊗_{R_x} g_x`, and since `g_x` is an isomorphism (a `J.W`-map is a stalkwise iso on

section WhiskerOfW

open MonoidalCategory CategoryTheory.Presheaf

variable {J : CategoryTheory.GrothendieckTopology C}

/-- **Whiskering a locally bijective morphism preserves local injectivity
(flatness-free).** For an *arbitrary* presheaf of modules `F` and a morphism `g`
whose underlying additive-presheaf map is locally bijective (`∈ J.W`), the
left-whiskered morphism `F ◁ g` is locally injective.

This is the single residual ingredient of the associator
`tensorObj_assoc_iso` under ROUTE (d). The mathematics is the stalkwise computation
`(F ◁ g)_x = id_{F_x} ⊗_{R_x} g_x`: a `J.W`-morphism on the topological site of `X`
is a *stalkwise isomorphism* (`TopCat.hasEnoughPoints` + the conservative-family
characterisation `hP.W_iff`, `Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Sites.Point.*`, 2026), and
tensoring an isomorphism `g_x` by `id_{F_x}` yields an isomorphism, so `F ◁ g` is a
stalkwise iso, hence locally bijective, hence locally injective — for *any* `F`,
needing NEITHER sectionwise flatness NOR local triviality of `F`.

The two Mathlib-side ingredients this stalkwise argument needs are
(d.1) the stalkwise characterisation of the module-level `J.W` on `Opens X` and
(d.2) the commutation `(A ⊗ᵖ B)_x ≅ A_x ⊗_{R_x} B_x` of the stalk with the
presheaf-of-modules tensor (stalk = filtered colimit, and `tensorLeft`/`tensorRight`
preserve filtered colimits over a module category). Neither ships at the
`PresheafOfModules` level at the pinned commit; porting them is the genuine residual
(analogist `ts-monoidal213.md`, Decision 3, porting ingredients d.1/d.2). Stated
here with its substantive intended type so the rest of the associator closes against

    IsLocallyInjective J ((toPresheaf _).map (F ◁ g)) := by

/-- **Flatness-free left-whiskering preserves the sheafification localizer.** The
ROUTE (d) replacement for `W_whiskerLeft_of_flat`: for an *arbitrary* `F` and a
locally bijective `g` (`∈ J.W`), the left-whiskered `F ◁ g` again lies in `J.W`.
Local surjectivity is free (`isLocallySurjective_whiskerLeft`, right-exactness);
local injectivity is the flatness-free stalkwise residual
`isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_W`. No flatness, no local triviality. -/
lemma W_whiskerLeft_of_W [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective Ab.{u}]
    (F : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))
    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N)
    (hg : J.W ((toPresheaf _).map g)) :
    J.W ((toPresheaf _).map (F ◁ g)) := by
  have hbij := hg
  rw [GrothendieckTopology.W_iff_isLocallyBijective] at hbij
  rw [GrothendieckTopology.W_iff_isLocallyBijective]
  exact ⟨isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_W F g hg,
    isLocallySurjective_whiskerLeft F g hbij.2⟩

/-- **Flatness-free right-whiskering preserves the sheafification localizer.** The
braiding-conjugate of `W_whiskerLeft_of_W`, mirroring `W_whiskerRight_of_flat`. -/
lemma W_whiskerRight_of_W [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective Ab.{u}]
    (F : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))
    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N)
    (hg : J.W ((toPresheaf _).map g)) :
    J.W ((toPresheaf _).map (g ▷ F)) := by
  have hwl := W_whiskerLeft_of_W F g hg
  have hconj : g ▷ F
      = (BraidedCategory.braiding M F).hom ≫ (F ◁ g) ≫ (BraidedCategory.braiding N F).inv := by
    rw [← Category.assoc, ← BraidedCategory.braiding_naturality_left g F, Category.assoc,
      Iso.hom_inv_id, Category.comp_id]
  rw [hconj, Functor.map_comp, Functor.map_comp]
  rw [(J.W).cancel_left_of_respectsIso, (J.W).cancel_right_of_respectsIso]
  exact hwl

end WhiskerOfW

### THE SINGLE RESIDUAL — `isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_W` (line ~411, typed sorry)
Statement (substantive, TRUE): for arbitrary `F` and `g` with `J.W (toPresheaf g)`
(locally bijective), `IsLocallyInjective J (F ◁ g)`.

**Why it is true (ROUTE (d), the Mathlib-blessed flatness-free technique):**
stalkwise `(F ◁ g)_x = id_{F_x} ⊗_{R_x} g_x`; a `J.W`-map on the topological site of
`X` is a *stalkwise isomorphism* (`TopCat.hasEnoughPoints` + `hP.W_iff`,
`Sites.Point.*`, 2026), and `id ⊗ (iso)` is an iso — so `F ◁ g` is a stalkwise iso,
hence locally bijective, hence locally injective. **No flatness, no local
triviality.** This is precisely why iters 206–212 were stuck: they split `J.W` into
inj+surj and tried to preserve *injectivity alone*, which genuinely needs flatness
(FALSE for invertibles over non-affine opens). Preserving the *combined iso* needs no
flatness — the root-cause fix the analogist `ts-monoidal213.md` identified.

# Residual: `PresheafOfModules.isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_W`

**Statement (TRUE).** Let `R : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat`, `J` a Grothendieck topology with
`J.WEqualsLocallyBijective Ab`. For an arbitrary `F : PresheafOfModules (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)`
and `g : M ⟶ N` with `J.W ((toPresheaf _).map g)` (locally bijective), the left-whisker
`F ◁ g` is locally injective: `IsLocallyInjective J (F ◁ g)`.

This is the SINGLE residual on which `tensorObj_assoc_iso` now depends (everything else
in the associator is closed, axiom-clean). Used (via `W_whiskerLeft_of_W` /
`W_whiskerRight_of_W`) only with `g = toSheafify` and `F = M.val`/`P.val`.

## Proof (ROUTE (d), Mathlib-blessed flatness-free technique)

The whole point: a `J.W`-morphism is a **stalkwise isomorphism**, and tensoring an
isomorphism by `id_F` is an isomorphism — so `F ◁ g` is a stalkwise iso, hence locally
bijective, hence locally injective. No flatness, no local triviality. (Contrast the
flat route `isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_flat`, which preserves injectivity ALONE
and so needs `Module.Flat.lTensor_exact` — false for invertibles over non-affine opens.)

Concretely on the topological site of a scheme `X` (`C = Opens X`,
`J = Opens.grothendieckTopology X`):

1. **(d.1) Module-`J.W` ⟺ stalkwise iso.** For `f` a morphism of presheaves of
   `R`-modules on `Opens X`, `J.W ((toPresheaf _).map f) ↔ ∀ x, IsIso ((stalkFunctor Ab x).map ((toPresheaf _).map f))`.
   - `J.W = locally bijective = locally injective ∧ locally surjective`
     (`WEqualsLocallyBijective`).
   - surjective half: bridge `CategoryTheory.Presheaf.IsLocallySurjective J` ⟺
     `TopCat.Presheaf.IsLocallySurjective` then
     `TopCat.Presheaf.locally_surjective_iff_surjective_on_stalks`.
   - injective half: the analogue for `IsLocallyInjective` (Mathlib has only the
     SEPARATED/sheaf version `app_injective_iff_stalkFunctor_map_injective`; needs the
     locally-injective ⟺ stalk-injective bridge for the `Opens X` topology).
   - Abstractly this is `ObjectProperty.IsConservativeFamilyOfPoints.W_iff` +
     `TopCat.hasEnoughPoints` (`Mathlib.Topology.Sheaves.Points`, 2026); the concrete
     stalk packaging is `TopCat.Presheaf.isIso_iff_stalkFunctor_map_iso`.

2. **(d.2) Stalk commutes with the relative module tensor.**
   `((A ⊗ᵖ B).presheaf).stalk x ≅ A.stalk x ⊗_{R.stalk x} B.stalk x`, naturally, so that
   `((F ◁ g).presheaf).stalk x = id_{F_x} ⊗ ((g).presheaf).stalk x` under the iso.
   - `(A ⊗ᵖ B).obj U = A.obj U ⊗_{R(U)} B.obj U` sectionwise
     (`PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj_obj`).
   - stalk = filtered colimit over `{U ∋ x}`; `tensorLeft`/`tensorRight` preserve
     filtered colimits over a module category; the ring varies, so this is
     "colimit of tensors ≅ tensor of colimits over the colimit ring". Genuinely
     missing at the `PresheafOfModules` level.

3. **Conclude.** `g_x := (toPresheaf g).stalk x` is iso by (d.1). By (d.2),
   `(F ◁ g)_x = id_{F_x} ⊗ g_x`, an iso (any functor preserves isos). So `F ◁ g` is a
   stalkwise iso, hence (d.1 again) `J.W ((toPresheaf _).map (F ◁ g))`, hence
   `IsLocallyInjective J (F ◁ g)` (`GrothendieckTopology.W.isLocallyInjective`).

## Status / cost

Both (d.1) and (d.2) verified Mathlib-absent at the `PresheafOfModules` level
(iter-213, LSP/leansearch). ~200–400 LOC stalk-infrastructure port. NOT a dead end —
the technique is Mathlib-blessed (`Sites.Point.IsMonoidalW`) and the hardest input
(enough points for `Opens X`) already ships. Recommend a dedicated infra-build
iteration OR USER escalation. Do not pivot the substrate design.

## Dead ends

- Pure section-level (route-c flavour without sub-site infra): the kernel of
  `id_F ⊗ g_U` is NOT generated by `F ⊗ ker(g_U)` without flatness (Tor₁ obstruction),
  so the flat sieve template has no flatness-free section-level swap.
- Route (c) sub-site restriction (`F|_V ≅ 𝒪_V` on a trivialising cover + right unitor):
  needs restriction-to-sub-site / over-category infra also absent; messier residual,
  and requires `IsLocallyTrivial` scoping that route (d) avoids entirely.
